items.title: Why Shares of American Express Are Falling Today
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: American Express CFO Christophe Le Caillec told investors that expectations for revenue in the first quarter of 2025 are 'too high' due to one less day in the quarter and a stronger dollar. However, management is confident in the company's full-year guidance for 8-10% revenue growth and earnings per share of $15-$15.50 in 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article presents a mixed outlook for American Express, with the company tempering near-term revenue growth expectations but maintaining confidence in its full-year guidance. This suggests a neutral sentiment, as the long-term prospects for the company remain positive, but there are some near-term headwinds.
